task_completion.description: The action sports image is opened in an image editing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screenshot shows an image editor (e.g., GIMP, Photoshop) open with the target action sports image loaded on the canvas.

task_completion.description: The motion blur filter or tool dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or tool settings panel specifically for 'Motion Blur' or 'Linear Blur' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The motion blur settings are configured for a horizontal direction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The angle in the motion blur settings is set to 0 degrees, 180 degrees, or the blur direction is clearly constrained to the horizontal (X) axis.

task_completion.description: The horizontal motion blur effect is visibly applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main image canvas shows a distinct horizontal smearing or blurring effect simulating left-to-right or right-to-left movement.

task_completion.description: The modified image is saved or exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save', 'Save As', or 'Export' dialog is visible, or there is a notification/file manager view confirming the blurred image has been saved to the disk.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image editor rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with a GUI application (like GIMP) rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick's 'convert'.

integrity.description: The agent edited the provided source image rather than downloading a pre-blurred image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being edited must match the original action sports photo, and there should be no evidence of web searches for 'blurred sports image'.

integrity.description: The blur effect was applied using the software's built-in tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of the motion blur dialog and real-time canvas updates, rather than a script execution or sudden file replacement.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is an action sports photo from Unsplash (ID: photo-1571019613454-1cb2f99b2d8b, 1000x667 pixels). To correctly apply a 'horizontal' motion blur, the blur angle in the image editor must be set to 0 or 180 degrees, or the X-axis blur length must be increased while the Y-axis remains at 0.
